name: Santa Fe Chili Appetizers
recipeInstructions: In a heavy skillet, crumble the lean ground beef and brown it without adding extra fat or oil. Add the chopped onions, bell pepper, and minced garlic to the pan and remove from heat. Cut the whole tomatoes into small pieces and add them to the meat and onion mixture along with the tomato juice. Rinse the kidney beans, drain thoroughly, and add them to the meat. Add the remaining ingredients: chili powder, cayenne pepper (or less), ground cumin, salt, paprika, bay leaf (broken in half), and Parmesan cheese (freshly grated). Mix well. Cover the skillet, bring to a boil and simmer over a low flame for 1 hour. Remove from heat. When the chili has cooled, add the Parmesan cheese. Follow the folding instructions for the triangle. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F until golden brown, about 20 minutes. Let cool a few minutes before serving.
